questions: 15 MCQs
LeetCode 1669: Merge In Between Linked Lists
Binary Tree Validation from preorder traversal
Rat maze variant problem
rounds.tips: Be strong in C/C++ programming, with solid fundamentals in Data Structures and OOPS concepts

questions: LeetCode 203: Remove linked list elements
Print all possible permutations of a given string
Technical questions on pointers & memory allocation in C
rounds.tips: C/C++ was preferred. Focus on communicating your approach clearly.

questions: OS topics: threads, system calls
Network-related scenario: choose best data structure
Project discussion
rounds.tips: Strong practical understanding of OS concepts essential. Be confident. Ask thoughtful questions.

key_tips: Be confident and answer clearly
Review past interview experiences and questions asked by the company
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't get stuck on syntax errors, focus on logic first
Don't include unfamiliar skills or technologies on your resume

rounds.tips: Only C, C++, Java allowed. Python is not allowed. Medium level questions.

questions: Majority questions on C programming and Operating Systems
One or two coding tasks
rounds.tips: Know about OS and C internals.

key_tips: Know about OS and C internals
mistakes_to_avoid: Make resume with emphasis on low level programming

questions: 15 MCQs (basic math, OS and OOP fundamentals)
Merge in between linked lists
Check if preorder traversal of binary tree represents a BST
Rat in a maze variant with jump parameter - find minimum moves to reach destination
rounds.tips: Keep fundamentals strong in C or C++. Practice regularly on LeetCode.

questions: Identify bugs in a C program dealing with recursion
Remove duplicate elements in a linked list
Reverse every word in a string representing a sentence
Theoretical concepts on trees and hashmap
OOP concepts
rounds.tips: Stay calm. Keep communicating with the interviewer. Be strong in C/C++. It's okay to say you don't know something.

questions: Propose a structure to store hierarchical employee organization
Write C-like pseudocode to print hierarchy for a specific employee ID (n-ary tree + stack)
Print k-largest elements in a BST
OS questions: memory management
rounds.tips: Ask questions aligned to the company's principles. Get to know about the company before interviews. Attend the Pre-Placement Talk.

key_tips: Be confident about what you already know
Revise regularly to avoid tense scenarios before interviews
Analyze past experiences from company archives
Practice self-introductions before physical interviews
mistakes_to_avoid: Include only familiar skills in resume
Do not get tensed during the interview

questions: 4 coding problems: 2 medium + 2 hard
Medium 1: Merge In Between Linked Lists (LeetCode)
Medium 2: Array with bit manipulation
Hard 1: Dynamic programming
Hard 2: Graph
rounds.tips: Practice LeetCode and GFG problems. Try to pass max test cases.

questions: Given a problem statement and algorithm — verify if it solves the question
Determine time complexity of given code snippet (while(k>0){ k/=10 })
Analyse and compare time/space complexity of two function implementations
Pair coding: application problem solved using hashmap O(n) time, O(1) space
rounds.tips: Revise DSA and core computer science topics.

questions: Difference between MongoDB and SQLite
Favourite programming language and why
Difference between C and Java
Polymorphism
Abstraction and why abstraction?
Difference between struct in C and class in Java
Explain time and space complexity using Binary Search (code it)
What is REST framework? Full form for REST?
What is LangChain? What are LLMs?
GET, POST methods usage in your project
Hackathon project questions: ML algorithms chosen and how problems were solved
rounds.tips: Be honest with your resume.

key_tips: Data Structures: Arrays, Linked Lists, Hashmaps, Graphs, Stacks, Queues
Algorithms: Sorting, Searching, Dynamic Programming, Graph Traversal (DFS, BFS)
Time & Space Complexity: Big-O notation, Optimization
OOP Concepts: Polymorphism, Abstraction
Database comparisons (e.g., MongoDB vs SQLite)
RESTful APIs: GET, POST, HTTP methods
Project discussion: tech stack, challenges, and solutions
Behavioral: Teamwork, problem-solving, past experiences
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't overcomplicate solutions; focus on simplicity and optimization
Ask clarifying questions instead of making assumptions
Avoid freezing if you get stuck — break down the problem
Ensure you can explain all aspects of your projects and tech stack

rounds.tips: Practice aptitude sections and review basic technical topics.

questions: Design a multi-functional interface for CAT machine operators: real-time safety, task estimation, unusual machine behavior detection, operator training suggestions, daily task dashboard
tips: Understand the problem statement thoroughly before coding
Make your idea unique, relevant, and impactful
Split tasks early based on team strengths
Use sample data for demos if real data isn't available
Plan your demo flow: short, clear, and impactful

questions: Why Java? Discussion on static, this, super keywords
Difference between regular for loop and enhanced for loop
Inheritance and its types
Diamond Problem and how Java handles it
Multilevel vs multiple inheritance
Write basic backend code
HTTP status codes
Contents of package.json file
Difference between List and Set; converting List to Set without iteration
Procedures and triggers in MySQL with example
Behavioral: learnings from club participation, why Caterpillar, short and long-term goals, peaceful life definition, openness to travel, why should we hire you
tips: Prepare a clear and confident self-introduction
Be strong in core technical concepts
Always ask something thoughtful when asked for questions

key_tips: Master core concepts and practice DSA regularly
Prepare for aptitude with IndiaBix and CareerRide
Gain hands-on experience through projects or hackathons
Always research the company and ask thoughtful questions
mistakes_to_avoid: Keep answers clear and to the point
Never add false information to your resume
Don't bluff through unfamiliar topics

rounds.tips: Practice timed aptitude questions. Focus on reading/comprehension. Review HTML, Java, Cloud, and OOP basics.

questions: Design a Smart Operator Assistant for Caterpillar machinery: task dashboard, real-time safety alerts, incident logging, operator training hub, unusual machine behavior detection, task duration estimation
rounds.tips: Focus on implementing strong, meaningful features over fancy UI. Be innovative and practical. Divide tasks based on team strengths.

questions: Project discussion: NoSQL vs SQL choice, challenges, most technically demanding project
Core Java: How to print a variable without creating an object, methods to compare two objects
DSA: Sort array so all zeros appear first while maintaining relative order of non-zero elements (input: {0,3,0,1,0,5,2}, output: {0,0,0,3,1,5,2})
SQL: Write a query and explain different types of joins
Frontend performance optimization techniques
rounds.tips: Be fully prepared to discuss everything on your resume. Revise Java, SQL, and Data Structures. Practice DSA regularly.

key_tips: Practice aptitude and core questions for OA
In hackathons: unique idea, plan time wisely, ensure all team members contribute
Be confident and strong in core concepts during interviews
Explain your projects clearly
mistakes_to_avoid: Thoroughly review your resume and avoid including anything you're not confident discussing

rounds.tips: Prepare basics well.

questions: Real-world problem statement given — build a fully functional solution in 24 hours
rounds.tips: Brainstorm ideas and work on requirements. Add a standout feature for the domain.

questions: Array problem: frequency count
Questions about API systems worked on
DBMS questions
OOP questions
rounds.tips: Learn about System Design and all projects in your resume. Explain issues you faced and how you handled them.

key_tips: Understand the problems you practice — they may not be the same but will be connected
Don't be nervous and never lie to the interviewer
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't be nervous during the process
Don't lie to the interviewer at any time

questions: 8 CS fundamentals questions (easy to medium, 10 min)
8 aptitude questions (easy to medium, 10 min)
~100-150 behavioral questions
rounds.tips: Try to be realistic in the behavioral questions.

questions: How would you write code in a scalable manner?
Project discussion (self-introduction included project details — impressed interviewer)
High-level abstract questions mostly around projects
rounds.tips: Include project info in self-introduction. Guide the interview toward your strong areas. It's okay to be yourself. Interviewer (Head of Cybersecurity) asked high-level, abstract questions. Value alignment with Chevron's values was heavily emphasized.

questions: Basic HR questions
Questions about Chevron and its competitors
Questions about points from the PPT/Pre-Placement Talk
rounds.tips: Know about Chevron's competitors and remember key points from PPT. Use project discussion to steer conversation if stuck.

key_tips: Be confident and the rest will fall in place
Value alignment with Chevron's values matters most
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't try to be idealistic

questions: Two coding questions (easy to medium)
MCQs on React.js and Angular
rounds.tips: Focus on passing test cases, even with brute force. OA relies on test cases passed.

Basics from Java, Python, and C
Internal implementation of HashMap in Java
rounds.tips: If you choose a language, master it including internal workings.

questions: Describe one of your projects and how to commercialize it
General questions about Citi
rounds.tips: Have good knowledge about your projects. Research the company well before the interview.

key_tips: Have a good understanding of the problems you practice
Don't be nervous and don't lie to the interviewer
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't be nervous during the process
Don't lie to the interviewer at any time

rounds.tips: Questions test arrays, strings, two pointers, algorithms, math. Also includes React and Angular sections.

questions: Deep dive into tech stacks used in projects
Python and DSA questions
Explain project from requirements analysis to deployment
rounds.tips: Know your tech stacks thoroughly inside and out.
Open talk round testing soft skills. Questions about hobbies and behavior.

key_tips: Be bold and confident on your answers
Answer in such a way that the interviewer cannot easily ask follow-up questions from your answer
mistakes_to_avoid: Avoid lying about your hobbies

resources_used: GeeksforGeeks Core Subjects Last Minutes Notes

questions: Aptitude questions
MCQs on core subjects
2 DSA questions
rounds.tips: Be strong with basics, solve problems regularly.

questions: Topic: A team of 10 people from an NGO. How do you plan and prepare in a war zone village with no internet connectivity?
rounds.tips: Listen carefully to others before adding your points. Do not rush to speak. Speak politely and with confidence.

questions: Design a database
SQL query to remove duplicates when primary key is unique but other attributes are duplicated
Explain a few algorithms at random
How to connect frontend to backend using Java and JDBC (with code)
rounds.tips: Be confident. Think aloud. Just tell what you think for the situation given. Don't try to exaggerate.

questions: How do you update yourself with current trends in technology?
What is brain rot?
What AI tools have you used? Pros and cons?
What's your opinion on AI — does it replace software engineers?
Discussion about hobbies and EPAM's work culture
rounds.tips: Be yourself and express yourself.

key_tips: Be consistent in problem solving and revising core subjects
mistakes_to_avoid: Do not write things you do not know in your resume

questions: Basic CS questions on OS, Computer Networks, and File Systems
One LeetCode-style Bit Manipulation question (True/False result)
15 MCQs for AI Developer role: latest LLM models, RAG, basic ML, architecture questions
rounds.tips: Do not cheat — AI monitors code and top scores were filtered for cheating. Use one-shot PDF/videos for last-minute revision. Partial credit possible by passing some test cases.

questions: Explain Chain of Thought reasoning and implement it for a use case on paper
rounds.tips: Explain projects in detail with key metrics (accuracy improvements, specific challenges). Be specific and use numbers. Control the narrative.

questions: If data is given as a CSV, how would you implement an LLM to retrieve data and answer queries? Optimize the solution.
rounds.tips: Focus is on whether you give up or not. Keep trying multiple approaches even if wrong. This round was conducted by the Head of AI.

questions: How does hashing work? Can you hash strings, integers, lists, and dictionaries?
Brief project discussion
rounds.tips: Take educated guesses and explain your reasoning. If unsure, say you think it's X because of Y. Ask the interviewer about their experience or the company's projects.

key_tips: Paste the job description into ChatGPT and ask for curated study resources
Go through all topics in the job description, don't skip any
It's alright if you're not an expert — just have some idea
mistakes_to_avoid: Do not dwell on a single topic for too long
Don't skip topics in the job description

questions: Section 1 (common): Technical questions on networks, databases, and one coding problem
Section 2: Frontend questions on React, HTML, and CSS
Section 3: Given a codebase for a web page plus target result — modify code (HTML/CSS) to match target (flex layout, box shadows, image styling)
rounds.tips: Even if you can't fully solve the coding problem, explain your approach through detailed comments and use functions to structure the code.

questions: Explain top two projects from resume
Concept of empathy in design
Differences between UI and UX
Difference between Flexbox and Grid
Challenges in collecting input data from websites using AI
How do you prepare before starting a design?
Showcase a deployed project
Write DOM structure and CSS for a layout sketched on paper
React hooks: useState, useEffect — roles and internal behavior
Custom hooks
Box-sizing in CSS
Redux and Context API
rounds.tips: Strong grasp of React, HTML, CSS. Be prepared to talk about your projects in detail.

questions: How do you handle pressure and respond in uncertain situations?
Willingness to relocate
rounds.tips: Stay calm and be honest about yourself.

questions: Why did you choose a career in UI?
Top 3 favorite and least favorite websites
Have you read any books on UI?
Specific questions about projects
rounds.tips: Be honest. Mention that you found the hiring process well-structured.

key_tips: Have a strong grasp of React, HTML, CSS
Be well-prepared to talk about your projects in detail
mistakes_to_avoid: Neglecting the basics
Unclear project explanations

questions: Remove the N-th node from the end of a linked list
rounds.tips: Be confident. Communicate your thought process to the interviewer clearly.

key_tips: Practice the Blind 75 list — these are mostly what get asked in interviews
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't stay silent — communicate throughout the interview while thinking

resources_used: LeetCode (~60 questions, mostly easy and medium)

questions: Find the top view of a binary tree (used BFS + HashMap tracking horizontal positions)
Time at college — personal journey discussion
Describe a time you disagreed with team members in a project
rounds.tips: Treat it as a conversation, not a Q&A. Add extra points and personal anecdotes. Ask about the internship and interviewer's experience too. Use STAR method for thinking process.

key_tips: Understand Microsoft's culture
Be relaxed — interviewers are managers who want to see if they'd enjoy working with you
Prepare fundamentals well and showcase your approach
They're interested in HOW you solve, not just the right answer
Accept feedback — don't blindly accept it, verify it and let them know
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't rush to the solution even if you know the answer — use STAR method
Don't give single-line answers in behavioral rounds — use anecdotes to control interview flow

rounds.tips: Keep stationary handy. Keep track of time.
Start with brute force and demonstrate optimization. Don't panic if wrong first time — use it as an opportunity to demonstrate thinking.
Be confident and genuine. Demonstrate knowledge of the firm. Ask thoughtful questions.

key_tips: Be confident in what you know
Stay positive and professional
mistakes_to_avoid: Avoid panicking if you get it wrong the first time — use it as an opportunity to demonstrate thinking

questions: Write code for a particular pattern
rounds.tips: Be strong with core concepts and vocabulary. Ensure your projects have an impressive and definite use case. Practice writing code on paper.

key_tips: Don't over-explain — keep answers clear, concise, and to the point
Strong communication: structure your thoughts before you speak
Confidence and clarity often matter more than lengthy explanations
mistakes_to_avoid: Avoid adding projects to your resume that you aren't thoroughly familiar with
Lack of clarity can leave a poor impression

questions: Find a pair of two numbers that add up to a target sum from a list of numbers
rounds.solution: Use a hash table to store the remaining value needed to satisfy the sum and break when found
rounds.tips: Be very thorough with projects in resume. Ensure skills are listed clearly. Highlight relevant experience for the role.
Rehearse to speak clearly. Ask relevant questions about the role and the organisation.

key_tips: Don't put things on the résumé unless you can explain and break down the details
mistakes_to_avoid: Do not try flukes with answers — be sure about the answer or say you're not sure
They don't mind when you honestly say you don't know

questions: Quantitative and Technical Aptitude
DSA question from Arrays
DSA question from Digit DP
rounds.tips: Strengthen core concepts and practice a mix of basic and advanced DSA problems.

questions: Advanced Java questions
React questions
DBMS questions including writing a query
Basic DSA questions
In-depth project discussion
rounds.tips: Stay confident even when not fully sure of an answer. It's okay to admit you don't know — explain your thought process. They test logical thinking, clear communication, and honesty.

questions: Self-introduction (hackathon experience triggered most follow-ups)
Hackathon: problem statement, team approach, individual contribution, outcome
How do you collaborate in a team, manage pressure, and make decisions?
HR: personal questions, situational scenarios, availability, relocation, interest in role
rounds.tips: Communication is crucial — not just answers but how clearly and confidently you explain. Be genuine, stay composed, maintain a positive and professional attitude.

key_tips: Stay confident throughout the process
Strengthen core concepts: aptitude, DSA, Java, React, DBMS
Be well-prepared to explain your project clearly
Communicate thought process effectively and be honest
Highlight real experiences like hackathons
mistakes_to_avoid: Focus on being clear and honest rather than having a perfect answer
Share real, specific experiences rather than general statements

rounds.tips: Prepare OS, Embedded Systems, and Advanced C programming.
Be familiar with what you put in your resume. PRO TIP: Have an internship experience!

key_tips: Resume plays a major role
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't bring an irrelevant resume to the interview
